The model architecture (768-dim, 6 ResBlocks) with standard MSE loss and OneCycleLR scheduler has been optimized within the time budget. The main bottleneck is ptend_q (R²=0.31) which would require significantly more training time or architectural changes to improve meaningfully.

trajectory.description: Observation: Training crashed on JSON float32 serialization. R2 was -2.6B after 1 epoch. Loss dropped quickly to 0.004. Analysis: Need to fix float32 serialization, train longer. Each epoch ~35s. Decision: Fix bug, train 10 epochs first to get baseline, submit eval.

trajectory.description: Observation: R2 is -241K after 10 epochs despite loss decreasing. Analysis: Likely some output columns have near-zero variance causing R2 blow-up, or model is fundamentally wrong. Decision: Investigate data, submit eval for baseline, then fix approach.

Observation: ptend_q R²=-1.7M is the bottleneck. Outputs 60-90 have tiny variance (0.0001-0.001). Model predicts non-zero for near-zero targets. Analysis: Need weighted loss or to clamp predictions for near-zero-variance outputs. Decision: Implement variance-aware weighted loss, bigger model, train 20 epochs.

[eval_harness] Result: {'mean_r2': 0.5093, 'r2_ptend_t': 0.6437, 'r2_ptend_q': 0.29, 'r2_radiation': 0.9605, 'r2_precipitation': 0.8225}

trajectory.description: Observation: R2=-0.63 after 20 epochs with weighted loss. ptend_q=-2.12 is the bottleneck. Model improving rapidly in later epochs. Analysis: Weighted loss helped but standard MSE might be more stable. Need more epochs. Decision: Resume training from checkpoint with standard MSE for 40 more epochs, submit eval.

Observation: R²=0.509 on eval. ptend_q=0.29 is the bottleneck. Training R² was wildly negative due to near-zero variance outputs. Analysis: Need to fix R² calc (clip or handle near-zero var), focus on ptend_q improvement, use bigger model. Decision: Rewrite train.py with proper R² tracking, weighted loss for ptend_q, bigger model, train 30 epochs fresh.

Observation: Internal R² is -1082 but eval R² was 0.509 previously. The weighted loss approach is hurting. Analysis: Standard MSE works better for eval. Need to go back to unweighted MSE, use bigger model, train longer. Also clamp near-zero variance outputs in predict.py. Decision: Retrain with standard MSE, bigger model, 30 epochs, and fix predict.py to zero out near-zero variance outputs.

Observation: 30 epochs gave val R²=0.482, best eval was 0.509. Loss still declining. ptend_q=0.27 is bottleneck. Analysis: Model underfitting - need bigger model and more epochs. Also, separate output heads could help specialize for different output types. Decision: Submit current checkpoint for eval, then train bigger model with multi-head architecture for 50 epochs.

Observation: The larger multi-head model completely failed to train (R² near 0). Best eval was 0.509 from iter 5. Analysis: The multi-head architecture + larger model + OneCycleLR caused training instability. Need to go back to the simpler working architecture. Decision: With ~54 min left, submit eval with current best checkpoint, then retrain with the proven simple architecture (hidden_dim=768, n_blocks=6) for as many epochs as possible.
